domain_goal: Expand AI infrastructure coverage across training, inference, orchestration, data/RAG, evaluation, security/governance/cost, hardware, and network/storage/cluster infrastructure while reusing local evidence before new crawling.

candidate_gaps: Add production incident/postmortem evidence and measured checkpoint restore benchmarks that include environment, recovery timing, and remediation context.
Add framework upgrade failure evidence beyond issue-level NCCL version/configuration reports and SGLang runtime dependency leads, especially non-PyTorch/DeepSpeed/Ray training field reports.
training-distributed.notes: NCCL remains source-backed communication and fabric evidence. Distributed Training Infrastructure covers framework lifecycle, PyTorch FSDP and Distributed Checkpoint, torchrun elastic restart, DeepSpeed ZeRO and checkpoint conversion, Megatron-LM project boundaries, Ray Train checkpoint/fault tolerance, and Kubeflow/KubeRay training job controller coverage. Task 3 adds local issue-level operational signals for NCCL failover/restart boundaries, ncclCommAbort hangs, NCCL version/RoCE configuration failures, a Ray separate-process H200/NVSwitch broadcast hang report, and SGLang runtime integration leads. Continuation parent 6 adds bounded AWS blog evidence: Outpost VFX DDP-on-EC2-P5/H100 case-study training with a G5-style single-GPU baseline, fixed hyperparameters, loss-threshold timing, up to 8x faster training, and 2-day v001 delivery versus 1-2 weeks; and SageMaker AI Blackwell/P6-B200 FSDP tuning guidance where activation checkpointing trades recomputation overhead for lower activation memory and larger batch-size headroom. The layer remains partial because these case-study and best-practice signals do not replace production postmortems, measured checkpoint restore benchmarks, exact benchmark submissions, or field reports with complete environment and remediation context.

candidate_gaps: Add KServe-specific autoscaling/canary evidence and production failure or rollback context for inference deployment controls after reliable primary-source capture; Ray Serve and Knative cover bounded autoscaling, placement, and traffic-split mechanics, while r9 KServe probes are blocked-source evidence rather than KServe behavior proof.

Add full production incident/postmortem evidence for inference serving with service impact, timeline, remediation, and follow-up ownership; current SGLang issue/PR records and r9 incident-source probes are incident-shaped or blocked evidence, not complete postmortems.

Add live trace/profiling artifacts tied to SLOs or alert thresholds, and decide whether selected SGLang scheduled crawler supplement leads need a full API refresh with joined comments and PR review comments.
inference-runtime.notes: SGLang and CUDA Green Context remain strong local runtime evidence, and inference-runtime has multi-project primary-source coverage for vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, Triton Inference Server, llama.cpp, ONNX Runtime GenAI, Ray Serve, Knative, and local TensorRT/vLLM runtime captures. R7 task 1 promotes local production-operations evidence: PegaFlow external KV-cache process boundaries, restart/rolling-upgrade implications, cache admission and HLL observability, vLLM rollout weight-sync and pause/resume controls, SGLang router request-id and abort-reason observability, PD/Mooncake failure handling, decode warmup readiness failures, DeepGEMM startup regression, KV-event publisher collisions, HiCache DRAM/SSD backend validation, and benchmark/profiling harness leads. R8 task 1 adds bounded primary-source deployment-control evidence for Ray Serve autoscaling, resource and accelerator placement inputs, placement-group/gang scheduling, Serve-controlled updates, and Knative autoscaling, immutable revisions, percentage traffic splits, blue/green rollout, and rollback-by-traffic-shift mechanics. R9 task 1 records duplicate/gap proof plus blocked-source notes for KServe autoscaling/canary/SLO trace and inference-serving postmortem probes; no KServe-specific or production postmortem claims were promoted because reliable primary-source content was not captured. The July 8 SGLang #24456 curation adds a page-level routed-experts return hot-path stall diagnosis. Parent-2 adds more bounded July 6-7 page-level SGLang scheduled crawler supplement evidence: NIXL/UCX disaggregated KV-transfer crash behavior, HiCache bootstrap-abort prefetch cleanup, KV-transfer metric-window semantics using prefill_kv_transfer_finish_time, Ascend 910B3 Mooncake Store buffer-registration failure, EAGLE DP-attention padded-state crash behavior with router retry and health check consequences, and GLM-5.1 MI350X sparse-MLA prefill profiling. Treat these as issue/PR page-level operational evidence, not merged-fix guarantees beyond page-visible merged state, not a full API refresh, not production postmortems, not SLO/alert thresholds, and not general benchmark baselines. The layer remains partial for KServe-specific behavior, production failure/rollback context, full production postmortems, live trace/SLO artifacts, and optional full SGLang API refresh. Continuation parent 8 adds source-visible Blackwell confidential-inference runtime evidence: secure work submission latency and reduced encrypted host-device bandwidth as CC overhead surfaces, FlashInfer CC-safe autotuner timing via the GPU global timer register, SGLang async D2H copy worker to move token readback off the scheduler critical path, SGLang piecewise CUDA graph replay for prefill and mixed batches, and the NVIDIA HGX B300/Qwen 3.5/SGLang CC Off versus On benchmark context. This is source benchmark context only, not a local benchmark baseline, SLO, MLCommons result, product ranking, or general performance guarantee.

candidate_gaps: Add production scheduling incident/postmortem evidence with service impact, environment, and remediation details.
Add Slurm-on-Kubernetes bridge deployment captures and scheduler benchmark evidence; local issue records are operational signals, not benchmark or deployment proof.
Add GPU operator and device-plugin upgrade failure/postmortem sources beyond local Kubernetes, Kueue, and Volcano issue examples.
orchestration-scheduling.notes: Kubernetes, Volcano, and Kueue remain covered by local closed-issue corpora with comment-completeness caveats. Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure covers Ray/KubeRay, Slurm GPU/GRES, Kubernetes device plugin, NVIDIA and AMD GPU operator, Kubeflow Trainer, and Kueue/ResourceQuota admission boundaries. Task 3 adds local issue-level evidence for Kueue admitted-but-unsuspended jobs, ResourceQuota admission risk, serialized preemption, ResourceFlavor-to-PyTorchJob placement, MultiKueue admission debugging, Volcano GPU gang scheduling, preemption, vGPU/device-plugin allocation, scheduler panic, and upgrade/quota failures, plus a downstream SGLang Slurm cgroup cleanup lead. Continuation parent 5 adds a bounded local DRA/device-plugin scheduling slice: Kubernetes #139016, #137617, #138882, #135661, #133702, #133488, #135901, and #138407 cover ResourceClaim/ResourceSlice stuck pods, gang scheduling, scheduler panic, CEL selector failure, kubelet ResourceClaim handling, and legacy device-plugin cleanup/state drift; Kueue #12207 covers DRA partitionable-device quota borrowing with OpenShift, Kueue Operator, NVIDIA GPU Operator, DRA Driver, and DRAPartitionableDevices context, while #9868 is only a feature-boundary record for DRA extended resources; Volcano #5119, #4692, #5335, #5361, and #2965 cover ResourceClaimTemplate preparation, DRA PreBind lock, vGPU over-subscription, Hami vGPU scale delay, and device-plugin reinstall OutOfSync scheduling boundaries. The layer remains partial for production incidents, Slurm-on-Kubernetes bridges, scheduler benchmarks, and operator/device-plugin upgrade postmortems with complete environment, impact, remediation, and ownership context.

candidate_gaps: Add full source-to-vector deletion and retention propagation run evidence that proves source deletes, permission changes, and retention events reach every embedding store, vector index, RAG cache, and evaluation dataset.

Add embedding model/version drift policies, retrieval-quality alerting, RAG data-pipeline cost attribution, and production incident evidence with environment, impact, timing, remediation, and ownership context.
data-rag-vector.notes: Milvus, Qdrant, Weaviate, pgvector, and FAISS official source notes provide primary coverage for vector database architecture, index tradeoffs, filtered retrieval, sparse retrieval, memory/on-disk placement, and embedding-index lifecycle. Data RAG Pipeline Infrastructure covers Ray Data batch/object-store pipelines, Flink checkpointed streaming refresh, Kafka Connect/Streams connector and stream-processor boundaries, Airflow/Dagster/Prefect workflow scheduling, TEI embedding worker boundaries, DataHub/OpenLineage metadata lineage, Iceberg/Delta/Hudi table lifecycle controls, and Langfuse RAG traces/evaluation. R7 task 2 adds bounded primary-source evidence for Milvus/Qdrant/Weaviate vector deletion and TTL controls, Azure AI Search source deletion detection, document-level RAG access metadata, chunk permission projection, PII masking, DataHub policy boundaries, Weaviate RBAC/tenancy, and Langfuse dataset versioning. The layer remains partial because local sources do not prove full end-to-end propagation across every source, vector store, cache, and evaluation dataset, and do not cover embedding drift policy, retrieval-quality alerting, cost attribution, or production incidents. R10 task 1 adds duplicate/gap proof and blocked-source evidence for the remaining run-level source-to-vector/cache/evaluation propagation, embedding drift policy, retrieval-quality alerting, and RAG cost-attribution gaps. Local evidence covers component mechanics, but no local or reachable source tied source events to embedding ids, vector/search updates, cache invalidation, evaluation dataset handling, timing, validation, alert routing, or cost reports.

candidate_gaps: Add service-specific production SLO definitions, validated alert thresholds, routing/escalation ownership, and incident/postmortem evidence for LLM platforms; current Prometheus, Alertmanager, Grafana, Grafana AI Observability, and r9 KServe/incident probe notes cover mechanics or blocked evidence, not production choices.
Add benchmark environment manifests with captured local results and compare them with trace/profiling artifacts before promoting benchmark claims.
Add TPU/XPU production observability and retention/access audit evidence around evaluation datasets beyond Langfuse dataset versioning.
eval-observability-reliability.notes: General eval-observability coverage includes primary source notes for OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, LangSmith and Phoenix tracing/evaluation workflows, Ragas and lm-evaluation-harness evaluation mechanics, NVIDIA DCGM/Nsight platform telemetry/profiling, and adjacent Langfuse evaluation dataset governance from r7 task 2. R7 task 3 adds bounded SLO and alerting mechanics from Prometheus alerting rules, Alertmanager routing, Grafana notification policies, Grafana SLOs, and Grafana AI Observability LLM metrics/alert examples, plus non-NVIDIA platform observability mechanics from AMD Device Metrics Exporter, ROCm/AMD SMI, and Intel Gaudi Prometheus Metric Exporter. R9 task 1 records that KServe serving trace/SLO and public inference-serving incident/postmortem probes did not yield reliable primary-source content in this sandbox. Parent-2 adds SGLang page-level observability leads for KV-transfer metric-window correctness and bounded MI350X GLM-5.1 sparse-MLA prefill profiling. Continuation parent 6 adds AWS Parallel Computing Service dashboard evidence using Amazon Managed Grafana, Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, CloudWatch Logs, Slurm/EFA/Node/DCGM exporters, and Jobs/Nodes/GPUs/Slurm/FSx/EFA/Logs dashboards. These are dashboard and metric-surface signals, not production SLOs, alert thresholds, routes, owners, incidents, or general benchmark baselines. The layer remains partial because local sources do not prove service-specific production SLOs, validated alert thresholds, routing ownership, incidents/postmortems, benchmark environment manifests with local results, TPU/XPU production observability, or evaluation dataset retention/access audit runs. R10 task 1 records blocked-source evidence for retrieval-quality alert routing/run evidence. Existing sources cover Ragas/Phoenix/LangSmith/Langfuse evaluation signals, OpenTelemetry GenAI, Grafana alert mechanics, and cluster dashboard mechanics, but not a service-specific retrieval-quality threshold, route, owner, and observed RAG run. Continuation parent 8 records bounded NVIDIA benchmark-context evidence for confidential inference: HGX B300 with Blackwell Ultra, Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B FP8, VM GPU passthrough, Intel TDX, SGLang server, CUDA 13.2, SGLang v0.5.12-cu130, NCCL v2.28.9-1, Docker plus NVIDIA Container Toolkit, 1024/1024 and 8192/1024 input/output lengths, and 4-256 concurrent requests. The source table reports throughput and median TPOT deltas versus CC Off; this remains vendor source context rather than a local result, validated alert threshold, service SLO, production postmortem, or MLCommons submission.

candidate_gaps: Add complete cross-cloud chargeback parity evidence that wires AWS/Azure/Google billing exports, Kubernetes namespace/workload labels, accelerator reservations or commitments, cloud account quotas, and team ownership into an actual reporting run.
Add production evidence for policy enforcement failures, delete/retention propagation failures, PII redaction failures, or remediation postmortems rather than only product documentation.
Add end-to-end policy-enforcement run evidence across source permissions, search/vector indexes, RAG caches, and evaluation datasets.
security-governance-cost.notes: Security Governance Cost Infrastructure covers Quantum InfiniBand tenant isolation, MIG/vGPU accelerator sharing, NVIDIA confidential-computing attestation, Kubernetes ResourceQuota, Kueue ClusterQueue/ResourceFlavor/cohort governance, OpenCost workload cost allocation, AWS accelerator capacity/service-quota planning, and NCCL cost-estimation boundaries. R7 task 2 adds bounded RAG governance coverage for Azure AI Search document-level access control, query-time security trimming, chunk permission projection, PII masking, DataHub metadata policy, Weaviate RBAC and tenant isolation, vector delete/TTL controls, and Langfuse evaluation dataset versioning. R8 task 3 adds bounded official-source coverage for AWS/Azure/Google Cloud cost allocation dimensions that can be normalized through tags, labels, namespaces, workloads, billing exports, and ownership metadata, plus Kubernetes ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, Kubernetes audit logging, Gatekeeper audit, Kyverno validation, and Kyverno PolicyReports for policy-as-code enforcement and audit/reporting mechanics. The layer remains partial because complete cross-cloud chargeback parity, full end-to-end policy-enforcement run evidence, production enforcement failures, delete/retention propagation failures, PII redaction failures, and incident/postmortem evidence are still thin. R10 task 1 records blocked-source evidence for RAG-specific cost reporting and end-to-end policy-enforcement runs. Existing sources cover OpenCost, cloud cost dimensions, RAG access/PII hooks, vector RBAC/tenancy, dataset versioning, and policy/audit mechanics, but not a joined RAG cost report or policy run across source permissions, search/vector indexes, caches, and evaluation datasets. Continuation parent 8 adds bounded local NVIDIA Blackwell confidential-inference evidence from the hardware-rooted AI security capture: hardware root of trust; fused private signing-key boundary; HGX B200/B300 multi-GPU confidential computing across up to 8 GPUs with NVLink encryption; NRAS remote attestation using GPU hardware report plus AMD SEV-SNP or Intel TDX CPU TEE measurements against RIM; model-decryption-key release after verified CVM state; and startup-only attestation latency. Treat this as security and inference trust-chain evidence, not legal-compliance completion, quota governance, production incident/postmortem evidence, or hardware SKU/catalog promotion.

candidate_gaps: Continue filling unresolved hardware fields only when product-specific pages, tables, datasheets, or PDFs provide source-backed single-card values.
Keep remaining ambiguous multi-variant rows such as Cambricon MLU370-S4/S8 unresolved until the source or catalog explicitly separates per-variant values.

Keep aggregate systems, cloud offerings, portfolio pages, discovery indexes, runtime-only evidence, H200 NVL, TF32, INT8 unit-boundary rows, Atlas 300I A2 CPU/cooling/fan/dimension/operating-temperature text, R200/R200-8F INT16, R200/R200-8F benchmark/training/inference-throughput claims, N2025 SR-IOV/RDMA/P4/protocol/storage-offload/line-rate/low-latency/benchmark/production-operation text, Asterfusion CX102S system switching capacity/gateway ports, Helium physical port variant aggregation/NFV/session/offload/storage-acceleration text, and benchmark/server/system claims out of single-card resolved fields unless the normalization boundary is explicit.

Keep Tiangai compute peaks, Tiangai memory bandwidth, TG150 form factor and host interface, benchmark parity, ecosystem compatibility, model-count performance, production-operation, training-throughput, and inference-throughput claims unresolved until a product-specific table, datasheet, PDF, or explicit source boundary exposes schema-supported values.

Keep MetaX C600 qualitative memory capacity/type/bandwidth, MetaX compute peaks, memory generation, memory bandwidth, host-interface lane count, virtualization guarantees, DeepSeek/model operation, ecosystem compatibility, benchmark, production-operation, application, server product, topology-scale, and supernode claims unresolved until source-visible values fit schema-supported single-product boundaries.

Keep JaguarMicro Yunxiao DPU RoCE v1/v2, iWARP, NVMe-oF, VLAN, VXLAN, GRE, Geneve, L2VPN, virtio acceleration, elastic network/storage, security isolation, QoS, hot-plug, live migration, hot upgrade, cloud-disk boot, memory, host-interface, power, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, inference-throughput, and aggregate-system claims as boundary evidence unless a product-specific schema-supported source exposes explicit normalized values.
hardware-accelerator.notes: The tracked July 6 compute accelerator baseline is reconciled: 43 raw files from commit bebfebd are cited or classified as duplicate/discovery-only evidence. R9 task 2 resolves a bounded single-card slice from existing local product-specific captures: Cambricon MLU370-X4, Cambricon MLU370-X8, and Kunlunxin RG800 now have structured SKU, observation, and resolved spec rows for visible compute, memory, host-interface, form-factor, interconnect where applicable, and power fields. R10 task 2 resolves a narrower Biren deployment-envelope slice from local product-specific captures: Biren Bili 106B, 106M, 166C, 166L, and 166M now have structured rows for only form factor and peak power. R10 task 3 resolves a Resnics DPU/SmartNIC and storage-offload slice: Stargate-N1025 now has resolved form factor, source-stated aggregate data-port bandwidth, memory capacity, host interface, and typical power; Stargate-S1100 now has resolved host interface, power, 4K/4-SSD read/write IOPS, and added-latency upper-bound fields. Continuation parent 1 adds Corigine Agilio CX 2x25GbE SmartNIC as a structured local product-brief slice with resolved form factor, aggregate 50 Gb/s data-port bandwidth, DDR3 memory type, 2 GB memory capacity, and PCIe Base 3.0 x8 host-interface wording. Continuation parent 3 adds Yusur K2-Pro DPU resolved form factor, 200 Gb/s source-stated network bandwidth, and 2 x PCIe Gen3 x16 host-interface fields, plus Yusur SWIFT-2200N Pro low-latency network DPU-card form factor. Continuation parent 4 expands the existing NVIDIA H200 SXM row with HBM3e memory type, 34 TFLOPS FP64, 67 TFLOPS FP32, 1,979 TFLOPS BFLOAT16, 1,979 TFLOPS FP16, 3,958 TFLOPS FP8, up to 700 W configurable TDP, SXM form factor, and NVIDIA NVLink 900GB/s plus PCIe Gen5 128GB/s interconnect from the local official H200 page, while preserving the existing 141 GB memory and 4.8 TB/s memory-bandwidth rows. Continuation parent 7 adds Resnics Stargate-N2025 DPU SmartNIC resolved form factor, aggregate 50 Gb/s data-port bandwidth from 2 x SFP28 25G Ethernet data ports, split-pool DDR4 memory capacity recorded as 24 GB from 8 GB FPGA logic memory plus 16 GB SoC logic memory, PCIe Gen4 x8 host interface, and 150 W power from the local Resnics product page. Continuation parent 9 adds Asterfusion Helium DPU SmartNIC resolved PCIe DPU SmartNIC form factor, source-stated 100 Gb/s mixed-service processing capability, expandable 64 GB memory, PCIe x8 Gen3.0/4.0 host interface, and 60 W source comparison power, plus Asterfusion CX102S-DPU module form factor and 8 GB DDR4 module memory inside a 1U open intelligent gateway. The layer remains partial for fields that require product-specific tables, datasheets, PDFs, or explicit per-card/per-variant normalization, including H200 NVL values, TF32, INT8 unit-boundary rows, FP64 Tensor Core as a separate metric, benchmark uplift, MIG, confidential-computing, NVIDIA AI Enterprise bundle, server/system options, H800 runtime mentions, Blackwell portfolio pages, Biren compute/memory values, broader DPU/SmartNIC variants, N2025 SR-IOV counts, RDMA queue-pair count, P4 flow-table/session/protocol/storage-offload details, line-rate forwarding, low-latency, benchmark, and production-operations claims, Yusur DOE lookup, NoC throughput, packet rate, NVMe-oF engine, unspecified 2M IOPS, SWIFT latency/jitter, power, benchmark, protocol, and production-operations claims, plus CX102S 72 Gb/s switching capacity, gateway port layout, one- or two-DPU system variants, and Helium physical port variant aggregation, NFV latency/session/offload/storage-acceleration, and cost-reduction text. Continuation parent 10 adds Kunlunxin R200 and R200-8F as separated product-spec rows from the local official R200 series page: both variants resolve full-height full-length dual-slot PCIe form factor, 256 TOPS INT8, 128 TFLOPS FP16, 32 TFLOPS FP32, GDDR6 memory type, 512 GB/s memory bandwidth, and PCIe Gen4 x16 host interface; R200 resolves 16 GB memory and 150 W power, while R200-8F resolves 32 GB memory and 160 W power. INT16, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, inference-throughput, and aggregate-system claims remain outside the resolved catalog fields. Continuation parent 11 adds Iluvatar Tiangai 100 and Tiangai 150 as bounded local product-page catalog rows. Tiangai 100 resolves full-length full-height dual-slot PCIe card form factor, 32 GB HBM2, PCIe Gen4.0 x16 lane host interface, source-stated 64 GB/s inter-chip bandwidth, and 250 W board power. Tiangai 150 resolves only 64 GB HBM memory and 350 W board power. Exact compute peaks, memory bandwidth, TG150 form factor, TG150 host interface, benchmark parity, model-count performance, ecosystem compatibility, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, and inference-throughput claims remain outside resolved catalog fields. Continuation parent 12 adds MetaX C500, C500X, C550, and C588 as bounded local product-page catalog rows. The four rows resolve only source-visible form factor, memory capacity, MetaXLink interconnect wording, and maximum power. Exact compute peaks, memory generation, memory bandwidth, host-interface lane count, virtualization guarantees, DeepSeek/model operation, ecosystem compatibility, benchmark, production-operation, server product, and supernode claims remain outside resolved catalog fields. Continuation parent 13 adds MetaX C600 as a bounded local product-page catalog row resolving only OAM 2.0 snap-in module form factor, MetaXLink/MetaXLink-E interconnect wording, and 1000 W maximum power. Qualitative memory capacity/type/bandwidth, compute, benchmark, production-operation, ecosystem, application, server, topology-scale, and 128-card supernode claims remain outside resolved catalog fields. Continuation parent 14 adds Huawei Atlas 300I A2 as separated 32 GB and 64 GB product-page variants. Both variants resolve dual-slot full-height full-length PCIe form factor, 560 TOPS INT8, 280 TFLOPS FP16, PCIe 5.0 host interface, and 350 W maximum power; the 32 GB variant resolves 0.8 TB/s memory bandwidth and the 64 GB variant resolves 1.6 TB/s memory bandwidth. CPU, passive air cooling, fan modules, dimensions, operating-temperature, server-drawer, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, inference-throughput, ranking, and aggregate-system claims remain outside resolved catalog fields. Continuation parent 15 adds JaguarMicro Yunxiao DPU as a bounded local announcement row resolving only aggregate 50 Gb/s Ethernet bandwidth from the source-stated 2 x 25G text; protocol, virtio, elastic network/storage, security, QoS, hot migration/upgrade, cloud-disk boot, memory, host-interface, power, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, inference-throughput, and aggregate-system claims remain boundary evidence.

candidate_gaps: Add complete production incident and postmortem evidence for EFA, RoCE/Spectrum-X, Lustre, WEKA, Ceph, and NVMe-oF failure modes in AI clusters, with impact, timeline, environment, remediation, and follow-up ownership.

Add exact product/provider benchmark submissions or case studies that tie storage/fabric choices to concrete training, checkpointing, and inference data paths with full environment, topology, configuration, measured results, and captured per-submission result artifacts.
Broaden non-AWS/non-NVIDIA fabric operations beyond AWS HyperPod/EFA mechanics, Trainium2 cloud-offering fields, existing Spectrum-X/RoCE evidence, and the r9 blocked MLCommons exact-result probe.
network-storage-cluster.notes: Network Storage Cluster Infrastructure now adds official source notes for AWS EFA, FSx for Lustre, WEKA, Ceph, SPDK NVMe-oF, AWS HyperPod topology-aware scheduling, EFA health checks, node replacement, checkpoint-aware resume, and MLCommons Storage benchmark mechanics, while reusing existing NCCL technical-blog fabric evidence and local DPU/SmartNIC/NVMe-oF product captures. R9 task 3 records blocked-source evidence for exact MLCommons Storage measured-result artifacts: local search found no captured per-submission result file, and bounded GitHub API/raw probes failed with local DNS resolution errors. R10 task 3 promotes Resnics Stargate-N1025 and Stargate-S1100 product-specific fields into the structured accelerator catalog while keeping S1100 IOPS/latency as card-level storage-offload values, not general NVMe-oF benchmark evidence. Continuation parent 1 keeps Corigine Agilio CX as product-specific SmartNIC catalog evidence for port, memory, and PCIe fields only. Continuation parent 3 keeps Yusur K2-Pro and SWIFT-2200N Pro as product-specific DPU/SmartNIC catalog or comparison evidence: K2-Pro 200 Gb/s and PCIe fields are catalog fields, while NVMe-oF engine, unspecified 2M IOPS, SWIFT latency, and jitter are not used as general storage-fabric, benchmark, operations, or SLO claims. Continuation parent 7 keeps Resnics Stargate-N2025 as product-specific DPU SmartNIC catalog evidence for form factor, aggregate data-port bandwidth, split DDR4 memory, PCIe Gen4 x8, and power only; its SR-IOV, RDMA, P4/session/protocol/storage-offload, line-rate, low-latency, benchmark, and production-operation text is not used as general storage-fabric, benchmark, operations, or SLO evidence. Continuation parent 9 keeps Asterfusion Helium and CX102S as product-specific DPU catalog evidence only: Helium resolved form factor, 100 Gb/s mixed-service processing capability, expandable memory, PCIe host interface, and source comparison power are not generalized to fabric performance; CX102S module memory is not generalized from the gateway's 72 Gb/s switching capacity, 16 x 1GE plus 2 x 10GE ports, or one/two-DPU system variants. Continuation parent 15 keeps JaguarMicro Yunxiao DPU as product-specific DPU catalog evidence for aggregate 50 Gb/s Ethernet bandwidth only; its RoCE/iWARP/NVMe-oF protocol list, tunnels, virtio acceleration, elastic storage/network, security, QoS, hot migration/upgrade, cloud-disk boot, benchmark, operations, and SLO wording is not generalized to storage-fabric behavior, benchmark, operations, or service guarantees. Continuation parent 6 adds AWS PCS cluster dashboard evidence for Slurm/EFA/Node/DCGM exporters, Managed Prometheus, Managed Grafana, CloudWatch Logs, and Jobs/Nodes/GPUs/Slurm/FSx/EFA/Logs views, plus Lambda/NVIDIA co-packaged optics planning evidence for Quantum-X Photonics Q3450-LD, 800G/GB300 NVL72 fabric power, reliability, cooling, fiber routing, and installation-procedure boundaries. These are dashboard and planning signals, not production incident/postmortem evidence, exact benchmark submissions, product rankings, or generalized SLO guarantees. The layer remains partial because public incident/postmortem sources, exact measured product/provider benchmark submissions, and broader non-AWS/non-NVIDIA fabric operations evidence are still thin.
